top_stories.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Let Any App Steal Account Tokens via Leftover Debug Flag
top_stories.summary: A debug flag left enabled in production builds of multiple Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled token-sharing restrictions, allowing any app on the same device to silently obtain the signed-in user's account token. The vulnerability enabled unauthorized access to email, files, calendar, and messaging without requiring credentials, a login prompt, or permission requests.

top_stories.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
top_stories.summary: A coding error in Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled a security setting designed to protect authentication, leaving accounts vulnerable to widespread takeover. The flaw affected apps including Word, PowerPoint, and Excel, enabling attackers to steal login credentials and data.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra pushes passkeys, tightens identity security
top_stories.summary: Microsoft has released multiple new identity and network access capabilities for its Entra product family over the past 30 days, with several features reaching general availability. The updates include passkey support and tightened identity security controls aimed at helping organizations implement zero trust security strategies.

top_stories.title: Hardening Microsoft 365 Against Token Theft
top_stories.summary: The article addresses hardening Microsoft 365 environments against token theft attacks, a critical authentication security concern. Token theft allows attackers to bypass MFA by stealing session tokens, making it a significant threat to identity and access management in M365 deployments.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra ID to enforce registered authentication methods for password resets
top_stories.summary: Microsoft is updating the Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) process in Entra ID to require explicitly registered authentication methods rather than allowing directory-stored contact attributes. This change means phone numbers or alternative email addresses stored in directory fields will no longer be usable for password resets unless they are also explicitly registered as authentication methods.

news.title: Attackers already know the secrets are on your developers’ machines. Do you?
news.summary: A GitGuardian analysis found an average of 150 secrets on a sample of developer endpoints, with private keys comprising 38% and cloud/identity provider/secret management credentials (including AWS IAM and HashiCorp Vault) making up 22% of unique secrets. The findings highlight credential sprawl on developer machines outside traditional security team visibility.

news.title: One-Click GitHub Dev Attack Lets Attackers Steal Full GitHub OAuth Tokens
news.summary: Researchers disclosed a one-click attack via Microsoft VS Code that exploits GitHub.dev to steal GitHub OAuth tokens with read/write repository access. The attack requires only a single link click to exfiltrate the OAuth token, affecting both public and private repositories.

news.title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
news.summary: The article discusses the concept of "Identity Dark Matter" in enterprise IAM environments, where identity activity occurs outside centralized visibility and control. It introduces Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP) as an approach to shrinking the IAM attack surface across fragmented identity ecosystems including machine identities and decentralized applications.

news.title: Unpatched Windows Search URI Vulnerability Lets Attackers Steal NTLMv2 Hashes
news.summary: Researchers at Huntress disclosed an unpatched Windows Search URI handler vulnerability that can expose NTLMv2 hashes to attackers. The flaw is similar to CVE-2026-33829, a spoofing vulnerability affecting the Windows Snipping Tool's ms-screensketch URI handler.

news.title: Azure Managed Identity Explained with Real-World Examples
news.summary: This article explains Azure Managed Identity, a feature that enables Azure resources to authenticate to other Azure services without storing credentials such as passwords, secrets, or connection strings. The content covers practical implementation examples of both system-assigned and user-assigned managed identities for secure resource access.

news.title: Preinstall to persistence: Inside the Red Hat npm Miasma credential-stealing campaign
news.summary: A supply chain attack compromised over 90 versions of @redhat-cloud-services npm packages, embedding malicious code in CI/CD environments and developer systems. The malicious code targets credential theft from GitHub, cloud platforms, and local machines, directly impacting identity and access security for affected development pipelines.

news.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
news.summary: The phishing-as-a-service platform Kali365, previously flagged by the FBI for targeting Microsoft 365, has expanded its targeting to include AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms. The platform employs device code phishing techniques to compromise identity and access credentials.

news.title: Dashlane Discloses Brute-Force Attack, Encrypted Vaults of Fewer Than 20 Users Downloaded
news.summary: Dashlane disclosed a brute-force attack in which an external threat actor targeted user accounts to bypass two-factor authentication, resulting in encrypted vaults being downloaded for fewer than 20 personal subscription users. The incident occurred on May 31, 2026, and involved attempts to break 2FA protections on affected accounts.

news.title: UPDATE: BIO-key Delivers Phishing-Resistant Biometric Security to Alabama’s AOD Federal Credit Union
news.summary: BIO-key has deployed phishing-resistant biometric authentication at Alabama's AOD Federal Credit Union through a partnership with IT solutions provider BlueAlly. The implementation delivers biometric security aimed at replacing traditional authentication methods vulnerable to phishing attacks.

news.title: Yubico YubiKey 5C NFC
news.summary: Yubico's YubiKey 5C NFC is reviewed as a hardware security key priced at $58 that supports multi-factor authentication. The device uses NFC and USB-C connectivity to serve as a second authentication factor for online accounts.

news.title: Identity Elevated: A New Unified Identity Experience in Cisco Cloud Control
news.summary: Cisco has launched a unified identity experience called "Identity in Cloud Control" within its Cisco Cloud Control platform. The solution provides visibility and management capabilities across human, non-human, and AI agent identities, and includes identity-driven agentic operations via an AI Canvas feature.

news.title: Zero trust physical security needs trust decisions at the edge
news.summary: Hikvision's VP of Global Information Security discusses the application of zero trust principles to physical security systems such as cameras and door controllers. The interview covers how trust decisions can be made at the edge of physical security infrastructure.

news.title: Building a secure auth code flow setup using AgentCore Gateway with MCP clients
news.summary: The article demonstrates how to implement OAuth authorization code flow for MCP servers hosted on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway. The guide covers configuring inbound authorization mechanisms for AI assistant access control using standard OAuth protocols.

news.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
news.summary: A security researcher published multiple zero-day exploits, prompting Microsoft to reportedly threaten criminal charges. The incident centers on Microsoft's legal response to unsanctioned vulnerability disclosure rather than a coordinated patch or CVE release.

news.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
news.summary: A supply chain attack campaign called Miasma has compromised Red Hat npm packages (@redhat-cloud-services) to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines. The attack employs install-time execution, credential harvesting, CI/CD targeting, encrypted exfiltration, and delivers a self-propagating worm.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
news.summary: A malicious npm package named "codexui-android" was discovered stealing OpenAI Codex authentication tokens as part of a supply chain attack, attracting over 29,000 weekly downloads before disclosure. The attack targets developers by masquerading as a legitimate remote web UI tool, harvesting authentication credentials from victims.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to bypass Instagram account security and reset passwords, successfully compromising high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force Chief Master Sergeant's profiles. The attack method was distributed via Telegram, instructing users how to manipulate the AI bot into performing unauthorized password resets, representing a social engineering vulnerability in AI-assisted authentication workflows.

news.title: Reference your own AWS Secrets Manager secrets in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Identity
news.summary: AWS announced that Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Identity now supports referencing secrets stored in AWS Secrets Manager, allowing users to use preconfigured secrets while retaining control over their management. This update pertains to credential and secrets management within AWS's AI agent identity service.

news.title: Extending MCP support for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway
news.summary: Amazon Web Services has extended Model Context Protocol (MCP) support for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway, incorporating access control and credential management features for AI agent deployments. The solution addresses enterprise requirements including fine-grained access control across MCP servers, observability into tool usage, and centralized credential management.

news.title: Pathlock Reinforces Its Leadership in ERP Security and Controls for the AI Era
news.summary: Pathlock has issued a press release positioning itself as a leader in ERP security and controls for the AI era. The announcement connects identity, governance, and assurance in the context of AI agents and automated processes affecting enterprise risk.

news.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
news.summary: Palo Alto Networks has disclosed an actively exploited authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-0257, CVSS 7.8) affecting PAN-OS GlobalProtect and Prisma Access. The flaw allows threat actors to bypass authentication and establish unauthorized VPN connections.

news.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
news.summary: Researchers discovered an exploit chain in a popular automation service that combined over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities to enable potential compromise. The attack chain highlights risks associated with excessive IAM permissions and non-human identity (NHI) management in complex cloud environments.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress are demanding answers while CISA works to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials to multiple highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted to conducting SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and enabled cryptocurrency theft from investors.
